archetype: Automated In-Situ Uranium Mine Remediation Monitoring and Control System
domain: Environmental Remediation of Uranium Mining Sites
description: This system monitors and controls the remediation of in-situ uranium mines, ensuring the removal of contaminants and restoring groundwater quality. It is used by environmental remediation teams and regulatory agencies. The system must handle complex hydrological models, real-time sensor data, and strict EPA regulations.
rules: MUST integrate with EPA's Comprehensive Environmental Response, Compensation, and Liability Act (CERCLA) guidelines
MUST comply with Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) standards for radiation safety
MUST support real-time data ingestion from sensors monitoring groundwater levels, pH, and contaminant concentrations
MUST perform hydrological modeling using MODFLOW or equivalent
MUST generate reports in EPA's Electronic Data Deliverable (EDD) format
anti_patterns: DO NOT use a simple CRUD-based data model, as it cannot capture complex hydrological relationships
DO NOT rely solely on manual data entry, as it can lead to errors and delays
DO NOT neglect to implement robust data validation and quality control measures
key_technical_challenges: Developing a scalable data ingestion pipeline for real-time sensor data
Integrating complex hydrological models with real-time monitoring data
Ensuring data security and compliance with EPA and NRC regulations

recommended_stack.language: Python, due to its extensive libraries for scientific computing and data analysis
recommended_stack.framework: Flask or Django, for building robust and scalable web applications
recommended_stack.database_engine: PostgreSQL, for its support of advanced data types and spatial queries
recommended_stack.cache: Redis, for caching frequently accessed data and improving performance
recommended_stack.message_broker: RabbitMQ, for handling real-time data ingestion and processing
